steps.expected: 8 spikes with mixed ratings and resolutions
steps.observed: File created with 8 spikes (4 blocking, 2 resolved, 2 positive)

steps.expected: Structured markdown with sections for blocking, hotspots, element notes
steps.observed: Output contains # 🎯 FEEDBACK INTEL header, BLOCKING ISSUES with 4 items, HOTSPOTS sorted by count, ELEMENT-SPECIFIC NOTES grouped by selector

outputSections: FEEDBACK HOTSPOTS with 4 selectors ranked by count

steps.expected: Structured markdown distinct from cursor-context with critical issues, hotspots, element feedback sections
steps.observed: Output contains # ⚡ SPIKES FEEDBACK REPORT header with different section organization

outputSections: CRITICAL ISSUES section with 4 blocking items

assertions.title: Context exports handle empty and positive-only data

steps.observed: cursor-context shows '✨ Clean slate! No blocking issues found' and 'No element feedback'

steps.action: Create workspace with positive-only spikes (love, like)

steps.observed: claude-context shows '✅ All clear. No critical issues blocking progress'

emptyStateMessages: cursor-context: '✨ Clean slate! No blocking issues found'
cursor-context: '📊 No element feedback. Nothing's hot yet'
claude-context: '✅ All clear. No critical issues blocking progress'
claude-context: 'No element hotspots. Reviewers haven't targeted specific elements yet'

assertions.title: Context exports correctly identify blocking feedback

steps.action: Verify blocking criteria in source code
Check fixture data against export output
steps.expected: 4 blocking spikes (unresolved meh/no), resolved negative excluded, positive excluded
steps.observed: Blocking section contains exactly: a4385-export-001 (no), a4385-export-002 (no), a4385-export-003 (meh), a4385-export-004 (meh). Excludes resolved a4385-export-005 (meh), resolved a4385-export-008 (no), and positive a4385-export-006/007

steps.expected: Punk/zine energy in section headers, not generic business language
steps.observed: Multiple punk/zine phrases identified in both formats

assertions.title: Context export tests exist and pass

steps.observed: 32 tests passed: format parsing, is_blocking logic, hotspots, empty states, positive-only, mixed ratings, element grouping, punk/zine tone, metadata

testCategories: ExportFormat parsing: 5 tests (json, csv, jsonl, cursor-context, claude-context, invalid format)
is_blocking logic: 7 tests (meh/no unresolved are blocking; love/like/resolved/no-rating are not)
hotspots: 4 tests (empty, page-only, single element, sorted descending)
cursor-context: 8 tests (empty state, positive-only, mixed ratings, resolved negative not blocking, element grouping, hotspots, punk tone, metadata)
claude-context: 7 tests (empty state, positive-only, mixed ratings, distinct from cursor, resolved marker, punk tone)

assertions.title: MCP tools and context exports use same data source

steps.action: Verify both use load_spikes() from storage.rs
steps.expected: Same data loading function called by both
steps.observed: export.rs calls load_spikes() at line 42; mcp.rs calls load_spikes() in get_spikes, get_element_feedback, get_hotspots

steps.action: Compare blocking criteria between export and MCP
steps.observed: Export: is_blocking() = !resolved && (Meh || No). MCP: unresolved_only filter excludes resolved; rating filter matches Meh/No separately

steps.expected: Both read from .spikes/feedback.jsonl via same storage function
steps.observed: storage.rs load_spikes() reads PROJECT_ROOT/.spikes/feedback.jsonl for both export and MCP

mcpFilteringLogic: unresolved_only: if true, filters out s.is_resolved()
rating filter: if provided, matches spike.rating against parsed Rating
evidence.blockingParity: MCP get_spikes with unresolved_only=true + rating='no' OR rating='meh' returns same subset as export blocking section. Both use is_resolved() from spike.rs and rating matching.

assertions.title: Export format error message lists all formats

steps.expected: Error lists all 5 supported formats, exit code 1
steps.observed: Error: Invalid format: invalid. Use json, csv, jsonl, cursor-context, or claude-context | Exit code: 1

evidence.errorMessage: Invalid format: invalid. Use json, csv, jsonl, cursor-context, or claude-context
evidence.testExists: test_invalid_format_lists_all_five in export.rs confirms all 5 formats in error message

summary: Tested 8 assertions: all 8 passed. cursor-context and claude-context formats produce valid markdown with correct structure. Blocking criteria correctly identifies unresolved meh/no spikes. Both formats handle empty and positive-only states gracefully. Punk/zine tone confirmed in headers. 32 export-related tests pass. MCP and export share the same data source (load_spikes) and blocking logic (unresolved + negative rating). Invalid format error correctly lists all 5 supported formats.
